parts.title: The Big Announcement
parts.text: Layla rushed into the classroom waving a bright orange flyer. The fourth grade was putting on a play about a parrot trapped in **captivity** who dreams of flying free. Benny grinned because he had always been **ambitious** about acting — he wanted every role to be perfect. Haruto, who loved music, offered to be the **composer** and write all the songs for the show.

parts.title: Getting Ready
parts.text: Every afternoon the three friends would **rehearse** their scenes in the gym. Layla worked hard to **concentrate** on remembering her lines while Benny practiced his moves across the stage. Haruto showed great **dedication**, staying late each day to finish a song that would **depict** the parrot's longing for the open sky. Their teacher smiled and said the play was shaping up to be a true **masterpiece**.

parts.title: Opening Night
parts.text: On opening night, the gym was packed with families. The bright, **vivid** costumes made the audience gasp. A large painted **portrait** of a jungle hung behind the stage, setting the scene. When the final curtain fell, the crowd leaped to their feet with roaring **applause**. Everyone agreed the show was quite a **spectacle** they would never forget.

parts.title: Looking Back
parts.text: After the curtain fell, Layla, Benny, and Haruto sat together backstage, still in their costumes. They laughed about the moment Benny almost tripped over a vine prop. Haruto said the best part was hearing the audience hum along to his songs. Layla nodded and said that working as a team made the whole experience unforgettable.

definitions.meaning1: To practice something, like a play or speech, before performing it. Example: The actors had to rehearse the scene five times before they got it right.
definitions.studentChallenge: We need to ___ our skit one more time before the talent show begins.

definitions.meaning1: The state of being kept in a place and not being free to leave. Example: The rescued bird had spent years in captivity before it was finally released.
definitions.studentChallenge: The zookeeper explained that the tiger had been born in ___.

definitions.meaning1: An impressive or eye-catching sight or event. Example: The fireworks display was a grand spectacle that lit up the entire night sky.
definitions.studentChallenge: The parade with all its floats and dancers was quite a ___.

definitions.meaning1: Clapping by a group of people to show they enjoyed something. Example: The singer smiled and bowed when she heard the loud applause from the crowd.
definitions.studentChallenge: The audience broke into ___ after the amazing magic trick.

definitions.meaning1: Having a strong desire to succeed or to do something great. Example: The ambitious student set a goal to read fifty books over the summer.
definitions.studentChallenge: She was ___ enough to try building a full robot for the science fair.

definitions.meaning1: A person who writes music. Example: The composer spent weeks writing a beautiful song for the orchestra to perform.
definitions.studentChallenge: Beethoven was a famous ___ who created many well-known symphonies.

definitions.meaning1: A work of art, music, or writing that is considered the best of its kind. Example: The painting in the museum was called a masterpiece because of its incredible detail.
definitions.studentChallenge: Everyone said the mural on the library wall was a true ___.

definitions.meaning1: The willingness to give a lot of time and effort to something important. Example: Her dedication to soccer practice helped her become the team captain.
definitions.meaning2: A message at the beginning of a book or song honoring someone. Example: The author wrote a dedication to her grandmother on the first page of her novel.
definitions.studentChallenge: His ___ to learning the piano was clear from the hours he spent practicing.

definitions.meaning1: To focus all your attention on something. Example: It is hard to concentrate on homework when the TV is on.
definitions.studentChallenge: Please turn down the music so I can ___ on my reading.

definitions.meaning1: To show or represent something in a picture, story, or other artwork. Example: The painting depicted a beautiful sunset over the mountains.
definitions.studentChallenge: The artist used bright colors to ___ a busy city street.

definitions.meaning1: Very bright, strong, and clear in color or detail. Example: The vivid rainbow stretched across the sky after the rain.
definitions.meaning2: Producing strong, clear images in the mind. Example: She gave a vivid description of her trip that made everyone feel like they were there.
definitions.studentChallenge: The sunset painted the sky in ___ shades of orange and pink.

definitions.meaning1: A painting, drawing, or photograph of a person, especially of the face. Example: The artist painted a portrait of the queen that hung in the palace.
definitions.studentChallenge: The photographer took a beautiful ___ of the family standing in the park.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The actors had to _______ their lines every day after school so they would be ready for the big show.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Before the talent show, the students decided to _______ one last time in the empty classroom.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The rescued eagle had spent many years in _______ before it was finally set free.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The story was about a princess held in _______ in a tall tower, waiting for a chance to escape.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The circus performers put on an amazing _______ with acrobats soaring through the air.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The giant ice sculpture in the town square was a _______ that drew visitors from miles away.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The crowd burst into _______ when the gymnast landed her final flip perfectly.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Thunderous _______ filled the theater after the orchestra played its last note.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ young inventor dreamed of building a machine that could clean the ocean.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Their plan to raise a thousand dollars for charity was _______, but they believed they could do it.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Mozart was a famous _______ who began writing music when he was only five years old.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The _______ played each note on the piano to make sure the new melody sounded just right.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The enormous sand castle was a _______ that took the whole family three hours to build.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Critics called the new movie a _______ because the acting and music were both outstanding.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Her _______ to volunteering at the animal shelter showed how much she cared about helping others.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The coach admired the player's _______ because she never missed a single practice all season.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She had to _______ hard during the spelling bee so she would not make a mistake.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: It was difficult to _______ on the math test because the hallway outside was so noisy.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The mural on the wall was meant to _______ the history of the town from its earliest days.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The author used detailed words to _______ the hero's journey through the enchanted forest.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ colors of the parrot's feathers made it the most beautiful bird in the zoo.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: He told such a _______ story about his camping trip that everyone could picture the starry sky.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The artist spent weeks painting a detailed _______ of the mayor to hang in city hall.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She drew a _______ of her best friend using colored pencils, and it looked just like her.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
Layla's class had an _(1)_ plan: they would create the greatest school play their town had ever seen. Their teacher asked Haruto, a talented young _(2)_, to write original music for every scene. The story would _(3)_ a magical bird trying to escape from _(4)_ and find its way home.

Every afternoon the students would _(5)_ in the gym, running through their lines again and again. Benny found it hard to _(6)_ at first because there was so much going on around him. But his _(7)_ paid off, and soon he knew every word by heart. Layla painted a giant _(8)_ of the bird's face to hang at the center of the stage.

On the night of the show, the _(9)_ costumes glowed under the bright lights. The audience watched in silence as the story unfolded into a true _(10)_. When the curtain fell, the gym shook with _(11)_. Everyone agreed the performance was the greatest _(12)_ the school had ever put on.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
